channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea says it tested hypersonic intermediate range missile aimed at remote Pacific targets
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Tuesday its latest weapons test was a new hypersonic intermediate-range missile designed to strike remote targets in the Pacific as leader Kim Jong Un vowed to further expand his collection of nuclear-capable weapons to counter rival nations.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico drops migrants in troubled resort as it disperses them far from US border
item.description: ACAPULCO, Mexico (AP) — About 100 migrants from various countries wandered directionless and disoriented through the streets of the troubled Pacific coast resort of Acapulco.
item.dc:creator: ANTONIO CASTILLO and MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Saudi Arabia presses Florida man to give up US citizenship over critical tweets, family says
item.description: The son of a Florida retiree says Saudi Arabia has forced his father to take steps to renounce his American citizenship after jailing him over social media posts critical of the kingdom’s crown prince.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Goodman And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada's Liberals look for a new prime minister as Trump threatens tariffs and an election looms
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation after facing an increasing loss of support both within his party and in the country.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Indonesia is admitted to the BRICS bloc of developing nations
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Indonesia has been admitted as a full member of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, the group's presiding country Brazil announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Departing US ambassador to Mexico sees 'major changes' under Trump in diplomatic relationship
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The outgoing U.S. ambassador to Mexico said Monday he anticipated “major changes” in the diplomatic relationship between the U.S. and Mexico during the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hurdles remain as Israel and Hamas once again inch toward a ceasefire deal
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas once again appear to be inching toward a ceasefire that could wind down the 15-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of Israelis held hostage there. Both Israel and Hamas are under pressure from outgoing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Congo to execute over 170 people convicted of armed robbery, official says
item.description: Over 170 inmates have been flown from the Democratic Republic of the Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to a high-security prison where they will be executed, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Foreign Affairs chair moving ICC sanctions bill amid Israeli arrest warrants
item.description: Rep. Brian Mast (R-Fla.), the new chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, is planning to move legislation this week to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC) after it issued an arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu last year.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated Maduro meets Biden at White House
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — The Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election met Monday with U.S. President Joe Biden just days ahead of Maduro's contested inauguration to a third term.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announces his resignation
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has announced his resignation, telling the nation that “internal battles” mean he “cannot be the best option” in the next election. Trudeau said Monday he has asked the president of his Liberal Party to begin the process to select a new leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis has named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, tapping an Italian nun
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Monday named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, appointing an Italian nun, Sister Simona Brambilla, to become prefect of the department responsible for all the Catholic Church's religious orders.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns ahead of 2025 elections
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau resigned Monday, ahead of general elections later this year in which the ruling Liberal Party is polling badly and amid tensions with President-elect Trump, who has threatened steep tariffs on the country over border policies.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia claims it captured another town in eastern Ukraine but Kyiv's troops say the fight continues
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian troops in eastern Ukraine have captured the stronghold of Kurakhove after a monthslong battle, Russia’s Defense Ministry claimed Monday, adding the wrecked town to the Kremlin’s list of conquests during the almost three-year war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Starmer slams 'lies and misinformation' after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has condemned “lies and misinformation” that he says are undermining U.K. democracy.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk extends political tentacles into UK, Germany
item.description: Elon Musk is seeking to wield the power of his social media network to boost far-right political movements in the United Kingdom and Germany, after becoming a major backer of President-elect Trump in November’s election.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea is a startling development, even for observers used to the country’s famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italy's Meloni makes surprise Mar-a-Lago visit to meet with Trump
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni made a surprise visit to Mar-a-Lago to meet with President-elect Trump on Saturday. They were joined by a number of Trump’s expected nominees for his forthcoming administration, according to a pool report, including Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Sunday that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader. “The Reform Party needs a new leader,” Musk said in a post on the social platform X, which he owns. “Farage doesn’t have what it takes.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Speculation grows that Austrian far right leader Herbert Kickl will be asked to form a government
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday announced that he would meet with far-right politician Herbert Kickl as speculation grows that he will ask the Freedom Party leader to form a government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Iran executes over 1K prisoners in 2024, highest total in 30 years, report says
item.description: A new report claimed Iran executed an unprecedented number of people in 2024. Most of the executions took place following the election of its new president in late July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ISIS increasingly unopposed following US withdrawal from Afghanistan, collapse of Syria
item.description: The Islamic State is seeing less resistance in areas of major security concern following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, the fall of the Assad regime in Syria and potential troop drawdown in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9
